steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to "specify" — to state exactly — the measure of magnesium relative to all elements on Earth. The notes provide a concrete number: magnesium's mass fraction is 154,000 ppm.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Magnesium's abundance on Earth is greater than zinc's, as reflected by magnesium's higher mass fraction.: the goal is to specify magnesium's value, not rank it against another element. ✗
  • Choice B — Magnesium's abundance can be expressed by a mass fraction, a measure of the element's mass relative to that of all elements on Earth.: without the specific ppm figure, the sentence does not "specify" anything — it stays vague. ✗
  • Choice C — The mass fraction of magnesium on Earth is 154,000 ppm.: states the exact ratio defined in the notes and names the precise value. ✓
  • Choice D — Magnesium's mass relative to the combined mass of all elements on Earth is 40 ppm.: uses the wrong element's number. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
